docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I have been a passionate developer since childhood, driven by a love for creating and innovating. My journey in programming has allowed me to bring ideas to life and make meaningful contributions to various communities.

Since 2019, I’ve been an active contributor to the Garry's Mod community, using my expertise to develop creative solutions and enhance player experiences. My work spans multiple frameworks, including Lilia, Nutscript, DarkRP, and Helix, where I specialize in crafting seamless integrations and innovative features.

With proficiency in both front-end and back-end development, I take a comprehensive approach to building robust, user-friendly applications. My commitment to excellence ensures that every project is dynamic, efficient, and tailored to the client's vision.

docs.title: Contact Me
docs.text:
Interested in collaborating on a project or just want to chat? Feel free to reach out through any of the following platforms:

  • Discord: liliaplayer
  • Steam: bititaleonheart
  • GmodStore: liliaplayer

docs.title: Admin Stick
docs.text: Description: Adds an SWEP to simplify player management. This SWEP can run administrative commands as well as chat commands targeted at players or entities. It aims to solve the problem of admins not knowing all the commands.

docs.title: Weapon Holstering
docs.text:
Description:

Allows players to holster and unholster weapons visually, improving immersion by showing the holstered weapon model on the player.

docs.title: Walk Types
docs.text:
Description:

A walk type mechanic similar to Star Citizen, allowing you to adjust your walking speed according to your needs. I added a table where you can set the speed values for each type as a percentage, making it easy to configure and balance. Sprinting, of course, overrides the walk speed.

docs.title: Intro Rules
docs.text:
Description:

A system that is planned to enforce that players review the rules before playing.

docs.title: Dropshipping System
docs.text:
Description:

Implements a Combine Dropship for fast travel or troop transport. Allows configuring destinations and straightforward setups.

docs.title: Faction Stashes
docs.text:
Description:

Provides a centralized stash shared by all members of a faction. Items can have a set price, managed by stock, and cannot be purchased if the stock is empty.

docs.title: Representative Voting
docs.text:
Description:

Implements a voting system akin to the American House of Representatives, where selected individuals from a predefined list are voted in for a given role or group.

docs.title: Legislative Voting
docs.text:
Description:

Introduces a lawmaking system where players propose laws and vote “Aye,” “Nay,” or “Abstain.” Results are tallied at the end.

docs.title: Presidential Voting
docs.text:
Description:

Facilitates presidential elections based on popular vote. Players register as candidates, and the system tallies votes.

docs.title: Citizen ID – Passport Papers
docs.text:
Description:

Designed for HL2RP, players start with temporary passport papers and must visit a Combine authority or terminal to obtain a permanent Citizen ID, each uniquely tied to the character.

docs.title: Working Terminal
docs.text:
Description:

Allows players to join or leave specific classes in a faction through interactive terminals. Settings are highly customizable, and players can flag themselves active or inactive.

docs.title: Code 99
docs.text:
Description:

Lets players quickly call police assistance via a keybind. Displays a waypoint to indicate how many officers are needed and highlights the location.

docs.title: Gas Mask
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a mustard gas SWEP and a gas mask item. Players in gas without a mask take heavy damage over time; wearing a gas mask provides immunity.

docs.title: RP Characters
docs.text:
Description:

Players spawn as randomly generated characters with unique stats. Upon death, the system shows how long they survived, adding narrative depth to roleplay.

docs.title: Zomboid Stats
docs.text:
Description:

Inspired by Project Zomboid, this adds detailed traits, skills, and backgrounds to characters. These stats can affect crafting, combat, and other gameplay elements.

docs.title: Radiation
docs.text:
Description:

Functions similarly to Fallout, where increasing radiation levels reduce maximum health. Anti-radiation items or treatments may be used to combat it.

docs.title: NPC Drop
docs.text:
Description:

Allows certain items to be dropped by certain NPCs depending on a given configuration.

docs.title: Roulette
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a gacha-like bag system that has an item table; items are given based on chance.

docs.title: Skill Menu
docs.text:
Description:

Provides a visually intuitive interface for players to view and improve their skills/abilities, useful in RPG-styled servers.

docs.title: View Bobbing
docs.text:
Description:

Introduces a more immersive first-person camera movement, making the player’s view gently sway while walking/running.

docs.title: Wiretaps
docs.text:
Description:

Lets players place wiretap devices in rooms and listen in from a remote receiver, facilitating covert surveillance.

docs.title: Rideable Mounts
docs.text:
Description:

Allows players to mount animals or creatures. Flying mounts can ascend by holding jump, while land mounts can sprint.

docs.title: In-depth Armors
docs.text:
Description:

Offers deep customization for armors, including movement speed changes, jump height, resistances, and visual overlays.

docs.title: Modified Character Creation | ID System
docs.text:
Description:

Adds an automatic description generator, including physical stats (height, weight) or attributes for new characters. May also provide an in-game ID system.

docs.title: Telephone
docs.text:
Description:

Adds payphones for public or private calls to other players; can be integrated with wiretapping.

docs.title: Jukeboxes
docs.text:
Description:

Allows environment-wide music playback using a configured library on a web server.

docs.title: Typewriter
docs.text:
Description:

Enables creation of in-game notes or documents linking to external Google Docs. Perfect for newspapers or official letters.

docs.title: Turns
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a Turns System that allows SeriousRP servers to manage turns in SeriousRP Scenarios.

docs.title: Forward Operating Bases (FOB)
docs.text:
Description:

Allows a faction to set up a spawnable outpost for its members to respawn at, with customizable cooldowns.

docs.title: Safebox
docs.text:
Description:

Works like an Ender Chest, giving players personal, persistent storage accessible from multiple safebox entities.

docs.title: Newspapers
docs.text:
Description:

Lets a designated faction create in-game newspapers with editable text, distributing them as physical items.

docs.title: Oil
docs.text:
Description:

Adds the ability to extract oil over time using rigs or pumps, which can then be sold or refined for profit.

docs.title: Revive System
docs.text:
Description:

Players become downed instead of dying immediately, giving teammates a chance to revive them before they bleed out.

docs.title: Vending Machines
docs.text:
Description:

Dispenses configurable items for a set price, automatically providing the purchased item to the player.

docs.title: Broadcast Radio
docs.text:
Description:

Allows server-wide voice or music broadcasts. Ideal for radio host or DJ-style roleplay.

docs.title: Vinyl Disk Player
docs.text:
Description:

Enables the use of vinyl records with unique tracks on a vintage-style player.

docs.title: Car Radios
docs.text:
Description:

Lets drivers and passengers tune into radio stations or streams while in a vehicle.

docs.title: Permits
docs.text:
Description:

Restricts certain items behind permits, which can be granted to specific factions or classes.

docs.title: Medical System
docs.text:
Description:

Provides detailed health mechanics such as limb injuries, diseases, and treatments.

docs.title: Clock
docs.text:
Description:

Displays current time, synchronizing with Stormfox2 or server time for roleplay purposes.

docs.title: Stables
docs.text:
Description:

Introduces a horse ownership system where players can purchase, spawn, and ride their personal horses.

docs.title: Magazines
docs.text:
Description:

Requires players to manage physical magazines for their weapons; guns only reload from these magazines.

docs.title: Perks
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a perks interface that can be restricted by faction, level, or other conditions.

docs.title: Simple Car Bomber
docs.text:
Description:

Lets players place a bomb in a vehicle, triggering a timed explosion with optional defusal mechanics.

docs.title: Factories
docs.text:
Description:

Spawns items automatically by consuming resources, acting like a production line with configurable rates.

docs.title: VATS
docs.text:
Description:

Inspired by Fallout’s targeting system, allows players to attempt targeted shots with chance-based success.

docs.title: Gacha Bags
docs.text:
Description:

Implements a gacha mechanic where players open “bags” or “boxes” for randomized item rewards.

docs.title: Airdrops
docs.text:
Description:

Periodically drops crates with loot around the map, encouraging players to race for the rewards.

docs.title: Turf Wars
docs.text:
Description:

Introduces capture zones that generate money or items for the faction holding them. Encourages territorial conflict.

docs.title: Animal Butchering
docs.text:
Description:

When certain animals are killed, their carcasses can be butchered for meat, hides, or other resources.

docs.title: Keycards
docs.text:
Description:

Grants item-based door access using keycards of varying clearance levels.

docs.title: Clearance Levels
docs.text:
Description:

Restricts doors and areas based on multi-tier security clearances, often tied to factions or ranks.

docs.title: Sanity
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a sanity meter that decreases from traumatic events, causing visual/auditory hallucinations at low levels.

docs.title: Organs
docs.text:
Description:

Assigns random-quality organs to each character. Better organs grant minor buffs; worse ones cause drawbacks.

docs.title: Attribute Rolling
docs.text:
Description:

Randomly generates character stats (e.g., Strength, Perception) on creation, affecting gameplay accordingly.

docs.title: Attribute Fog
docs.text:
Description:

Limits view distance based on a “Perception” attribute, creating literal fog for lower scores.

docs.title: Cinematic Text
docs.text:
Description:

Displays large, stylized text overlays for dramatic effect during events or cutscenes.

docs.title: Attribute-Based Damage
docs.text:
Description:

Ties damage output to specific stats, such as Strength for melee.

docs.title: Durability
docs.text:
Description:

Implements weapon durability, reducing an item’s effectiveness over time until repaired.

docs.title: Weapon Wheel
docs.text:
Description:

A weapon wheel that can be summoned by holding the middle mouse button. Clicking on any weapon equips it. I added support for logos, if necessary.

docs.title: Squads
docs.text:
Description:

Allows players to form squads (or parties) with shared chat, markers, and possible group rewards.

docs.title: Reputation System
docs.text:
Description:

NPC factions may be friendly or hostile depending on the player’s faction affiliation, gear, or actions.

docs.title: Karma System
docs.text:
Description:

Awards or subtracts karma based on player actions, which can affect NPC or faction attitudes.

docs.title: Faction Menu
docs.text:
Description:

Enables in-game faction management for inviting or removing members.

docs.title: Map
docs.text:
Description:

Provides a top-down map interface.

docs.title: Serial Numbers
docs.text:
Description:

Each item (or weapon) has a serial number that police can check. Some can be erased if illicit.

docs.title: Director Spawner
docs.text:
Description:

Introduces a Director Spawner, similar to the system used in Left 4 Dead. Zombies spawn within a specified range, limited by both quantity and environmental conditions, creating dynamic and strategic gameplay.

docs.title: Transformative Items
docs.text:
Description:

Allows items to temporarily or permanently turn the player into a creature with unique abilities.

docs.title: Clothing Status
docs.text:
Description:

Tracks clothing cleanliness. Over time, clothes get dirty and may need washing or repair.

docs.title: Raiding System
docs.text:
Description:

Adds structured raiding or ambushing features for faction conflict. Notifies defenders and rewards attackers or defenders.

docs.title: Police Lockers
docs.text:
Description:

Police officers can temporarily draw guns from lockers while on duty and must return them afterwards.

docs.title: TV System
docs.text:
Description:

Allows broadcasting of live or recorded content to in-game televisions for other players to watch.

docs.title: JMOD EFT
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with JMOD EFT, bringing EFT-inspired features—loot containers, advanced medical/ballistic items, and more.

docs.title: Infection
docs.text:
Description:

When attacked by zombie players or zombie NPCs, an infection mechanic is triggered. As your infection level increases, you begin to experience more debilitating debuffs. You can cure yourself with Zombrex, but if your infection surpasses 100%, you will fully transform into a zombie.

docs.title: Ranking System
docs.text:
Description:

Adds visible ranks on the scoreboard. Can integrate with factions, awarding custom weapons or models for higher ranks.

docs.title: Syringes
docs.text:
Description:

One-use items that grant temporary boosts (speed, armor, health, or jump). Often used in combat or emergencies.

docs.title: Social Credit System
docs.text:
Description:

Awards points for desirable behavior or tasks. Points can unlock perks, titles, or bonuses.

docs.title: Farming
docs.text:
Description:

Enables planting and growing crops over time, harvested for resources or sale.

docs.title: Advanced Injuries
docs.text:
Description:

Implements various injuries (bleeding, broken limbs, PTSD, concussion) with unique effects and specialized treatments.

docs.title: Guilds
docs.text:
Description:

Allows creation of player-made organizations with custom ranks, permissions, shared banks, and logs.

docs.title: Assigned Housing
docs.text:
Description:

Enables properties or doors to be assigned to specific factions or players, with persistent ownership and rent.

docs.title: Law Board
docs.text:
Description:

Presents a clickable board that opens a webpage or text displaying server rules or local laws.

docs.title: DEFCON
docs.text:
Description:

Implements security levels 1–5, shifting server states or restrictions based on threats.

docs.title: Trading
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a secure trade window to exchange items and money, preventing scams.

docs.title: Cutscenes
docs.text:
Description:

Creates Call of Duty-inspired mission briefings or story intros, complete with cinematic camera transitions.

docs.title: Cinematic Entrance
docs.text:
Description:

Emulates Call of Duty–style mission starts or teleports. Players may spawn or enter a zone with dramatic camera work.

docs.title: Cassette Player
docs.text:
Description:

Simulates an old-school cassette deck that can play localized music or audio logs. Great for thematic servers wanting a retro vibe.

docs.title: Consoles
docs.text:
Description:

Generates in-game “consoles” or terminals that periodically provide random roleplay information, data logs, or intel. Often used in sci-fi or military settings.

docs.title: Global Voice
docs.text:
Description:

Allows admins or specially permitted users to broadcast voice chat server-wide, overriding normal proximity or radio channels.

docs.title: Intel
docs.text:
Description:

Provides randomizable “intel” items or data that can be found, traded, or used in missions. Often has strategic value for factions or roleplay storylines.

docs.title: Limb Ripper
docs.text:
Description:

A more extreme take on limb damage, expanding upon limb severing with additional gore effects.

docs.title: Activities
docs.text:
Description:

Cycles through daily or weekly tasks for players to complete, rewarding items or currency.

docs.title: Combat Zones
docs.text:
Description:

Introduces designated PvP or contested areas, rewarding players or factions for maintaining control.

docs.title: NPC Relationship
docs.text:
Description:

Makes NPCs hostile or friendly based on faction alignment. Enemy factions attack on sight; allied ones assist.

docs.title: NPC Spawner
docs.text:
Description:

Places spawn points that periodically generate NPCs of selected types, with configurable rates and behaviors.

docs.title: Waypoints
docs.text:
Description:

Creates navigational markers with on-screen indicators or distance previews.

docs.title: Daily Rewards
docs.text:
Description:

Offers login streak bonuses. Missing a day resets the streak.

docs.title: Steam Group Rewards
docs.text:
Description:

Provides extra benefits to players who are verified members of the server’s Steam group.

docs.title: Steam Name Rewards
docs.text:
Description:

Grants in-game perks or items if players include the server name/tag in their Steam username.

docs.title: Character List
docs.text:
Description:

Lets admins or players view all characters tied to a SteamID. Can show stats, offline ban options, or other management tools.

docs.title: ⚠️ Disclaimer
docs.text: Note: This compatibility is meant for non-DarkRP based frameworks.

docs.title: Modern Warfare Base Support
docs.text:
Description:

Implements compatibility with the Modern Warfare Weapon Base.

docs.title: ARC9 Base
docs.text:
Description:

Integrates the ARC9 weapon customization system.

docs.title: ARCCW Base
docs.text:
Description:

Implements a compatibility system for ARCCW, similar to ARC9 but focused on ARCCW’s codebase.

docs.title: GQuest
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with GQuest.

docs.title: Zero's Retro Mining
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with Zero's Retrominer (Mining Script).

docs.title: ATOW/CW Compatibility
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with ATOW and Customizable Weaponry.

docs.title: TFA
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with TFA Base.

docs.title: APhone
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with APhone.

docs.title: Zero's Cooking
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with Zero's MasterChef.

docs.title: Zero's Factory
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with Zero's Factory.

docs.title: CityWorker
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with CityWorker.

docs.title: CH_Farming
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with CH_Farming.

docs.title: CH_BitMiners
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with CH_BitMiners.

docs.title: Fine/Fining
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with Fine/Fining System Customizable.

docs.title: LSCS - Lightsaber
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with [LSCS] - Lightsaber - Force Powers.

docs.title: Simple Mining System
docs.text:
Description:

Adds compatibility with Simple Mining System.

docs.title: Bodyguard System
docs.text:
Description:

Enables hiring NPC guards for protection, with wages and loyalty checks.

docs.title: Property Seller
docs.text:
Description:

A NPC that rents houses to players.

docs.title: Black Market Vendor
docs.text:
Description:

NPC offering illegal items or deliveries to specific map locations.

docs.title: Car Dealer
docs.text:
Description:

Facilitates buying and selling cars through an NPC menu, optionally with customization.

docs.title: Bounty Hunter
docs.text:
Description:

Provides bounties for wanted players. Rewards are given once proof of capture or elimination is provided.

docs.title: Jailer
docs.text:
Description:

Allows jailing of players for a set time through an NPC interface, automatically freeing them after.

docs.title: Mechanic
docs.text:
Description:

Hires players into a mechanic faction, repairing vehicles for money.

docs.title: Police
docs.text:
Description:

NPC that manages police faction recruitment and handles crime reports or warrants.

docs.title: Taxis
docs.text:
Description:

Lets players join a Taxi Job, picking up NPC or player passengers for monetary rewards. Comes with an Automatic Taxi feature, which allows to fast travel to configurable locations for a fee.

docs.title: Delivery
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a delivery job where players drive packages around the map, earning money upon completion.

docs.title: Recruitment
docs.text:
Description:

Instantly recruits players into a specific faction with assigned ranks if necessary.

docs.title: Help NPC
docs.text:
Description:

Offers new or existing players in-game tips and tutorial info, possibly linking to external resources.

docs.title: EFT HUD
docs.text:
Description:

Mimics the Escape from Tarkov HUD, showing minimalistic health, ammo, and stamina bars on-screen.

docs.title: Fallout 76 HUD
docs.text:
Description:

Reproduces the Fallout 76 interface, including AP, health, rad meters, and the signature aesthetic.

docs.title: Start Info
docs.text:
Description:

Displays a custom panel on character creation to explain the server setting and lore.

docs.title: Fallout 3/New Vegas HUD
docs.text:
Description:

Provides a classic green Pip-Boy style HUD, showing health, AP, rad levels, and ammo counters.

docs.title: Video Intro
docs.text:
Description:

Adds an introductory video (or series of videos) that plays for new players, conveying rules, lore, or tutorials. The system supports various triggers—such as first-time login or entering specific areas—and seamlessly transitions back to gameplay afterward.

docs.title: Warhammer Character Sheet
docs.text:
Description:

Adds a dedicated Warhammer-themed character sheet that references Warhammer lore. This may include specialized factions, weapons, or races, integrated with unique stats and roleplay elements to create an immersive Warhammer experience.

docs.title: Achievements
docs.text: Achievement system tracking kills (zombies, players, NPCs), item pickups, playtime, deaths, and multi-objective challenges with rewards

docs.title: Animations
docs.text: Roleplay animations (/act commands) including surrender, salute, cross arms, attention, typing poses, and combat stances that interrupt on movement

docs.title: ArcCW Attachments & Magazines
docs.text: Creates inventory items for all ArcCW weapon attachments and magazines with configurable capacity and ammo types

docs.title: Armor
docs.text: Adds Funny Armors

docs.title: Banking
docs.text: Full banking system with multiple accounts, deposits/withdrawals, transfers, item storage, check writing/redemption, ATMs, and paycheck integration

docs.title: Black Market NPC
docs.text: Provides features such as Sells illegal weapons and contraband, Limited stock with restock timers, Requires players to find secret location, Prices configurable via script, and Great for underground roleplay.

docs.title: Cameras
docs.text: Security camera and CCTV monitor system for surveillance with faction-restricted access and live feed viewing

docs.title: Car Bomb
docs.text: Terrorism mechanics with vehicle car bombs (engine-activated) and door explosives, both with timed detonation, remote detonation, and debris creation

docs.title: Car Dealer
docs.text: Provides features such as Purchase vehicles from a dealer NPC, Return or repair owned vehicles for a fee, Custom paint jobs and bodygroup modifications with configurable price, Garages store vehicles safely, and Integrates with driving addons.

docs.title: Car Options
docs.text: Vehicle quality of life features including CD player system with music tracks, albums, and audio streaming for in-car entertainment

docs.title: Car Spawner
docs.text: Module for spawning themed cars for players.

docs.title: Chess and Droughts
docs.text: Interactive chess and checkers (draughts) board games with multiplayer support, wagering system, and leaderboard tracking

docs.title: Clearance Levels
docs.text: Adds clearance level system to doors

docs.title: Clothing System
docs.text: Bonemerge clothing system attaching hats, glasses, shirts, pants, shoes, ties, vests, rings, and accessories to players with vendor purchase and persistence

docs.title: Corpses & Looting
docs.text: Corpse looting system allowing players to search dead bodies, transfer items and money, with configurable search distance and anti-spam protection

docs.title: Crafting
docs.text: Recipe-based crafting system with crafting stations (forge, workbench), time-based progress, attribute requirements, tool dependencies, and faction restrictions

docs.title: Daily Rewards
docs.text: Daily rewards system with 20 objectives (walking, chatting, playtime, money, interactions, jobs, building, vehicles, arrests, healing, property, lockpicking, paychecks, ads, emergencies, XP, voting, crimes, spending, sprinting) and escalating streak bonuses at 14/30/60/90 days.

docs.title: Death Trooper Scrambler
docs.text: Communication scrambler system for Death Trooper faction allowing private, encrypted voice communications with sound effects for non-authorized listeners

docs.title: Delivery NPC
docs.text: Provides features such as Offers parcel delivery side jobs, Random drop-off locations per mission, Pays players money on completion, NPC shows delivery progress via dialog, and Encourages exploration of the map.

docs.title: Drugs
docs.text: Drug cultivation, processing, consumption, and addiction system with effects like speed boost, damage reduction, and stamina regeneration

docs.title: Dynamic Food
docs.text: Restaurant cooking system with cooking machines, customer orders, ingredient fridges, worktables, dish preparation, and hunger restoration mechanics

docs.title: Events
docs.text: Map-based NPC event system with configurable spawn positions, player count requirements, automatic timers, and staff/player notifications

docs.title: Extraction
docs.text: Raid-style extraction system requiring players to reach escape zones within countdown timer, with configurable positions per map and audio/visual cues

docs.title: Fence NPC
docs.text: Provides features such as Buy stolen items from players, Pay based on item reward values, Support for all robbery items, and Secure transaction system.

docs.title: Gathering System
docs.text: Resource gathering from trees (spruce, sticks, sap) and rocks (iron/gold/silver ore, coal), fishing system with pole/bait and fish types (trout, bass, catfish, perch) plus junk, crafting materials (ingots, wood, swords), and stackable inventory management.

docs.title: Help NPCs
docs.text: Provides features such as Provides information to new players, Can give basic starter items, Dialog tree explains server mechanics, Directs players to other NPC services, and Simple friendly conversation.

docs.title: ID System
docs.text: ID card system allowing players to show/request/force view IDs with multiple designs (California, German, Miami, New York, NYPD, Southside, Yorkshire) and character recognition mechanics.

docs.title: Improved Constructable Props
docs.text: Constructable prop system with furniture items (boxes, cabinets, chairs, tables, couches) requiring resources and supporting upgrades into functional entities

docs.title: LSCS Compatibility
docs.text: Adds compatibility with LSCS (Lightsaber Combat System) for enhanced lightsaber combat mechanics.

docs.title: Leveling
docs.text: Character leveling system with XP, skill points, and skill tree (Endurance: +25 health, Combat Training: +15% damage, Engineering: +15% tool effectiveness/+30% repair speed) with dependencies, F1 menu integration, and admin adjustment tools.

docs.title: Limb Damage
docs.text: Limb damage system where leg shots cause configurable sprint delay with automatic healing and toggleable enable/disable functionality

docs.title: Lockpicking
docs.text: Door lockpicking system with 4 difficulty levels (Basic, Copper Easy, Silver Medium, Gold Hard), job restrictions (thieves can lockpick, police cannot), and configurable success margins with interactive mini-game

docs.title: Lootables
docs.text: Searchable loot containers (cardboard boxes, wooden crates, metal barrels, lockers, safes) with rarity tiers (Common/Uncommon/Rare/Legendary/Unique), skill requirements (strength/luck), alarm systems, and cooldown timers for exploration-based item gathering.

docs.title: Marketplace
docs.text: Provides features such as Marketplace system for player-to-player trading, Buy and sell items with no expiration dates, Configurable listing limits and minimum prices, NPC vendor interface for easy access, Search and filter functionality for items, Economic system integration for server economy, and Super admin item removal tools.

docs.title: Mechanic NPC
docs.text: Provides features such as Repairs damaged vehicles for a fee, Can customize vehicle paint jobs, Sells spare parts and upgrades, Works with car dealer garage system, and Uses simple NPC dialog.

docs.title: Medals
docs.text: Persistent medal award system displayed on character profiles with staff permissions for giving/taking medals and admin controls

docs.title: Mobile Phones
docs.text: Mobile phone system with unique phone numbers, voice calling, text messaging, and contact management for player communication

docs.title: Police Suite
docs.text: Comprehensive police system with alarms, 999 emergency calls, stun weapons, crime tracking, warrants, arrests, police NPCs, and computer database for law enforcement management.

docs.title: Radio Voice Chat
docs.text: Handheld radio voice chat system with frequency tuning, encrypted channels, faction access, static radios, and range-based communication

docs.title: Ranking
docs.text: Ranking system for the gamemode

docs.title: Recruitment NPC
docs.text: Provides features such as Recruits players into factions, Provides introductory dialogue, Can assign starter gear upon enlistment, Supports multiple factions if configured, and Simple interaction with yes/no options.

docs.title: Referrals
docs.text: Player referral system allowing players to refer others and earn monetary rewards when referred players join, with configurable reward amounts and admin management tools.

docs.title: Roleplay NPCs
docs.text: Roleplay NPC framework with dialogue trees, quest systems, and submodule support (mechanics, taxis, vendors, fences) with centralized NPC management and customization tools.

docs.title: Roleplay NPCs
docs.text: Provides features such as Allows players to buy and sell properties, NPC manages rental durations and payments, Staff privileges for managing listings, Works with property submodule of NPCs, and Integrated showcase for property system.

docs.title: Store Robbery
docs.text: Store robbery system with jewelry cases and cash registers, stealable items (cash, jewelry, watches, rings), police requirements, and respawn timers

docs.title: Taxi NPC

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all achievement system configurations in the Achievements module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Achievements module. The achievement system allows players to earn achievements by completing various objectives, with rewards for successful completion.

docs.title: Achievements Table
docs.text: The MODULE.Achievements table defines all available achievements that players can earn. Each achievement has objectives, requirements, and rewards.

docs.title: How Achievements Work
docs.text: Achievements track player progress toward specific goals. When players complete the required objectives, they receive the achievement and its associated reward. Achievements can track kills, deaths, item pickups, playtime, or multiple objectives.

docs.title: Achievement Structure
docs.text: Each achievement contains: - name: Display name shown to players - description: Explanation of what the achievement requires - requirement: Target number that must be reached - reward: What players receive upon completion - type: Type of objective (kill, death, item_pickup, playtime, multi_objective) - entity: What entity/entities count toward the achievement (for kill types)

docs.title: Kill Type (kill)
docs.text:
Tracks kills of specific entities. Players must kill the required number of entities to complete the achievement.

Example Structure: 

["achievement_id"] = {
    name = "Achievement Name",
    description = "Kill X entities",
    requirement = 10,
    reward = "Reward description",
    type = "kill",
    entity = "npc_zombie"  -- Single entity
    -- OR
    entity = {              -- Multiple entities
        ["npc_fastzombie"] = true,
        ["player"] = true
    }
}

docs.title: Death Type (death)
docs.text:
Tracks player deaths. Players must die the required number of times to complete the achievement.

Example Structure: 

["achievement_id"] = {
    name = "Achievement Name",
    description = "Die X times",
    requirement = 1,
    reward = "Reward description",
    type = "death"
}

docs.title: Item Pickup Type (item_pickup)
docs.text:
Tracks items picked up by players. Players must pick up the required number of items to complete the achievement.

Example Structure: 

["achievement_id"] = {
    name = "Achievement Name",
    description = "Pick up X items",
    requirement = 10,
    reward = "Reward description",
    type = "item_pickup"
}

docs.title: Playtime Type (playtime)
docs.text:
Tracks how long players have been playing. Players must play for the required number of seconds to complete the achievement.

Example Structure: 

["achievement_id"] = {
    name = "Achievement Name",
    description = "Play for X time",
    requirement = 3600,  -- Seconds (1 hour)
    reward = "Reward description",
    type = "playtime"
}

docs.title: Multi-Objective Type (multi_objective)
docs.text:
Requires players to complete multiple different objectives. All objectives must be completed to earn the achievement.

Example Structure: 

["achievement_id"] = {
    name = "Achievement Name",
    description = "Complete all objectives",
    reward = "Reward description",
    type = "multi_objective",
    objectives = {
        {
            id = "objective_id",
            name = "Objective Name",
            description = "Objective description",
            requirement = 25,
            type = "kill",
            entity = "npc_zombie"
        },
        -- More objectives...
    }
}

docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Achievements module for tracking player progress, rewards, and achievement-related events.

Overview

The Achievements module provides a comprehensive achievement system that tracks various player activities including kills, item pickups, playtime, deaths, and multi-objective challenges. It includes reward systems, progress tracking, and integration with the server's economy and notification systems. The module provides extensive hook support for customizing achievement behavior, reward distribution, and event handling.

docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all ARCCW weapon system configurations in the ARCCW module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the ARCCW module. The ARCCW module provides integration with ARCCW weapons, including magazine system support and weapon configuration.

docs.title: ARCCWMagazinesEnabled
docs.text:
• Description: Whether the magazine system for ARCCW weapons is enabled
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: true
  • Category: ARCCW
  • Purpose: When enabled, ARCCW weapons use a magazine system where players must reload magazines separately. When disabled, weapons use standard reload mechanics.

docs.title: ARCCWMagazineCapacity
docs.text:
• Description: Default magazine capacity for generated magazines
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 30
  • Minimum: 1
  • Maximum: 100
  • Category: ARCCW
  • Purpose: Sets the default number of rounds that fit in a magazine when magazines are automatically generated

docs.title: ARCCWMagazineModel
docs.text:
• Description: Default 3D model path for generated magazines
  • Type: String (Generic)
  • Default: "models/Items/BoxSRounds.mdl"
  • Category: ARCCW
  • Purpose: Model displayed for magazine items in the inventory and world

docs.title: Magazine System
docs.text:
When ARCCWMagazinesEnabled is set to true, ARCCW weapons use a magazine-based reloading system:

  • Players must carry separate magazine items
  • Magazines can be loaded with ammunition
  • Reloading consumes a magazine from inventory
  • Empty magazines can be refilled with ammunition
  • Magazine capacity is determined by ARCCWMagazineCapacity unless overridden per weapon

When ARCCWMagazinesEnabled is set to false, weapons use standard reload mechanics without separate magazine items.

docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all armor system configurations in the Armor module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Armor module. The armor system allows players to equip different armor sets that provide protection, modify movement speed, and change player appearance.

docs.title: Armor List
docs.text: The lia.armors.list table defines all available armor sets that players can equip. Each armor has properties that affect gameplay, appearance, and player capabilities.

docs.title: Armor Properties
docs.text:
Each armor entry contains the following properties:

  • name: Display name shown to players
  • desc: Description explaining what the armor is
  • icon: Model path used for the armor icon in menus
  • model: Player model path that replaces the player's appearance
  • footstep: Sound file path for custom footstep sounds (nil for default)
  • jumpBoost: Percentage change to jump height (negative = lower jumps)
  • speedBoost: Percentage change to movement speed (negative = slower movement)
  • resistance: Damage reduction percentage (0-100)
  • fallDamage: Whether the player takes fall damage (true/false)
  • powerArmor: Whether this is power armor (affects certain systems)
  • overlay: Image path for screen overlay effects (nil for none). The overlay system is available by default and can be used with any armor configuration, not just the pre-configured armors listed below.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Banking module. The banking system provides bank accounts, deposits, withdrawals, transfers, item storage, check writing, and ATM functionality.

docs.title: Bank Account Slots
docs.text: The lia.banking.BankAccounts table defines available bank account slots. Each slot has a price and VIP requirement.

docs.title: Account Slot Properties
docs.text: Each account slot contains: - price: Cost in currency units to purchase this account slot - vip: Whether VIP status is required to purchase this slot

docs.title: Available Account Slots
docs.text:
• Slot 1: Price $1,000, VIP not required
  • Slot 2: Price $1,000, VIP not required
  • Slot 3: Price $1,000, VIP not required
  • Slot 4: Price $1,000, VIP required
  • Slot 5: Price $1,000, VIP required

docs.title: Check Types
docs.text: The lia.banking.checkTypes table defines all available check types that players can write. Each check type has visual properties and optional faction restrictions.

docs.title: Check Type Properties
docs.text: Each check type contains: - name: Display name shown to players - desc: Description of the check - overlay: Image path for check overlay (optional) - overlayAlpha: Transparency value for overlay (0-255) - overlayColor: Color applied to overlay (RGB Color) - factions: Array of faction IDs that can use this check (empty table = no restrictions)

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

The Black Market NPC module implements an underground contraband trading system that allows players to purchase illegal weapons, items, and contraband through hidden black market dealers. Players must locate secret black market NPCs and can place orders for various illicit goods with realistic import delays, risk mechanics, and delivery systems that encourage exploration and underground roleplay scenarios.

Main Features
  • Hidden NPCs Black market NPCs teleport between locations
  • Basket Orders Buy multiple items in one order
  • Import Delay Items arrive after configurable delay timer
  • Random Drops Items spawn at random locations or player position
  • Cooldown System Prevents repeated orders with cooldown timer
  • Waypoint System Automatic waypoint to package location
  • Package Expiration Packages despawn after time limit
  • Proximity Check Must be near NPC to place orders

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all blackmarket system configurations in the Blackmarket NPC submodule.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Blackmarket NPC submodule. The blackmarket system allows players to import illegal items through NPCs, with configurable locations, cooldowns, and item availability based on faction.

docs.title: ImportTime
docs.text:
• Description: Time in seconds required to import an item (0 = instant)
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 0
  • Purpose: Controls how long players must wait for imported items to arrive

docs.title: DespawnTime
docs.text:
• Description: Time in seconds before imported items despawn if not collected
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 1800 (30 minutes)
  • Purpose: Prevents imported items from accumulating indefinitely

docs.title: ImportingCooldown
docs.text:
• Description: Global cooldown in seconds between any imports for a player
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 600 (10 minutes)
  • Purpose: Prevents players from importing items too frequently

docs.title: randomPosition
docs.text:
• Description: Whether imported items spawn at random positions within drop locations
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: false
  • Purpose: When true, items spawn at random positions. When false, items spawn at exact drop location coordinates.

docs.title: locationChangeTime
docs.text:
• Description: Time in seconds before blackmarket NPC locations change (if location rotation is enabled)
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 5
  • Purpose: Controls how often NPC locations rotate (if rotation system is implemented)

docs.title: Locations Configuration
docs.text: The lia.blackmarket.Locations table defines blackmarket NPC spawn locations and item drop locations organized by map.

docs.title: Location Properties
docs.text:
lia.blackmarket.Locations = {
    ["map_name"] = {
        DropLocations = {
            ["Location Name"] = Vector(x, y, z)
            -- OR for multiple positions:
            ["Location Name"] = {Vector(x1, y1, z1), Vector(x2, y2, z2)}
        },
        NPCLocations = {
            ["Location Name"] = {
                pos = Vector(x, y, z),
                ang = Angle(pitch, yaw, roll)
            }
        }
    }
}

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Bonemerge clothing system attaching hats, glasses, shirts, pants, shoes, ties, vests, rings, and accessories to players with vendor purchase and persistence.

Main Features
  • Clothing Slots Hats, glasses, shirts, pants, shoes, ties, vests, rings, neck, wrist, face, fulloutfit
  • Bone Attachment Attaches clothing models to player bones
  • Gender Restrictions Male and female specific clothing
  • Slot Conflicts Cannot wear multiple items in same slot
  • Compatibility System CanWearWith checks for item compatibility
  • Vendor NPCs Buy clothing from NPC vendors with whitelists
  • Inventory Removal Items removed from inventory when equipped
  • Character Persistence Equipped clothes saved to character data
  • Auto Spawn Items spawn when unequipped if inventory full

docs.title: About

About

The Car Dealer NPC module provides a comprehensive vehicle purchasing and management system that allows players to buy, customize, repair, and store vehicles through specialized car dealer NPCs. Players can browse available vehicles, purchase them with realistic pricing, customize paint jobs and modifications, and utilize secure garage storage facilities for their automotive assets.

Main Features
  • Vehicle Purchase Buy vehicles from dealer NPC
  • Vehicle Return Return owned vehicles for refund
  • Repair Service Repair damaged vehicles for a fee
  • Paint Jobs Customize vehicle colors and finishes
  • Bodygroups Modify vehicle bodygroups and parts
  • Garage Storage Store vehicles safely in garages
  • Addon Support Works with driving addons

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Car Dealer NPC submodule. The car dealer system allows players to purchase, sell, spawn, and manage vehicles with configurable prices, repair costs, insurance, and customization options.

docs.title: Vehicles Configuration
docs.text: The lia.cardealer.Vehicles table defines all vehicles available for purchase, their properties, and associated costs.

docs.title: Vehicle Properties
docs.text:
Each vehicle entry contains the following fields:

  • Name: Display name shown to players
  • Category: Vehicle category/group (e.g., "Muscle Cars", "HL2", "Commercial", "Classic")
  • Price: Purchase price in currency units
  • factions: Array of faction IDs that can purchase this vehicle (empty table = no restrictions)
  • vip: Whether VIP status is required to purchase
  • allowColorChange: Whether players can change vehicle color
  • allowBodygroupChange: Whether players can change vehicle bodygroups
  • noBuy: Whether vehicle can be purchased (true = cannot buy, used for admin/test vehicles)
  • PaintJobPrice: Cost to change vehicle's paint job
  • RepairTime: Time in seconds required to repair vehicle
  • RepairCost: Cost in currency units to repair vehicle
  • InsuranceCost: Cost in currency units to purchase insurance
  • InsuranceReduction: Percentage reduction in repair costs when insured (0.5 = 50% reduction)

docs.title: Vehicle Structure
docs.text:
lia.cardealer.Vehicles["vehicle_class"] = {
    Name = "Vehicle Name",
    Category = "Category Name",
    Price = 25000,
    factions = {},
    vip = false,
    allowColorChange = true,
    allowBodygroupChange = true,
    noBuy = false,
    PaintJobPrice = 300,
    RepairTime = 75,
    RepairCost = 75,
    InsuranceCost = 250,
    InsuranceReduction = 0.5
}

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all configuration settings in the Car Spawner module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Car Spawner module. The car spawner system allows players to purchase and spawn themed vehicles through item crates.

docs.title: AvailableCars
docs.text:
• Description: Table defining all available vehicles that can be spawned through the car spawner system
  • Type: Table
  • Default: See example below
  • Purpose: Defines vehicle spawner items that players can purchase and use to spawn vehicles
  • Usage: Set in module.lua as MODULE.AvailableCars = {...}

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
Each entry in the table uses the vehicle's unique ID as the key and contains:

  • name (string): Display name of the vehicle shown to players
  • description (string): Description text explaining what the vehicle is
  • price (number): Cost in money to purchase the vehicle spawner item

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
MODULE.AvailableCars = {
    ["lvs_wheeldrive_dodspaehwagen"] = {
        name = "Dodge Paehwagen",
        description = "A powerful car that can be used for transportation.",
        price = 10000,
    },
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Each vehicle entry automatically creates a spawner item with the base class base_vehiclespawner
  • The spawner item uses the model models/props_junk/wood_crate001a.mdl by default
  • Items are automatically registered when modules are initialized
  • The carType property is set to the unique ID for vehicle spawning logic

docs.title: Cinematic Scenes
docs.text: The lia.cinematics.scenes table defines all welcome scenes shown to new players. Each scene has title, subtitle, description, camera settings, and duration.

docs.title: Scene Properties
docs.text: Each scene contains: - title: Main title text displayed - subtitle: Subtitle text displayed below title - description: Description text explaining the scene - camera: Camera position and angle settings - pos: Vector position (x, y, z) - ang: Angle rotation (pitch, yaw, roll) - fov: Field of view value - duration: Duration in seconds (uses sceneDuration if not specified)

About

Hierarchical security clearance system with 7-tier access control for doors, character management, salary bonuses, and administrative oversight.

Main Features
  • Seven Tiers Clearance levels 0 through 6 stored per character
  • Door Clearance Doors require clearance level stored in database
  • Door Hacking Radial minigame to hack doors with cooldown timer
  • Salary Bonuses Clearance adds bonus pay to character salary
  • Item Restrictions Items can require clearance to equip
  • Hack Warnings Players with warned clearance notified of hack attempts
  • Database Storage Door clearance saved per gamemode and map
  • Admin Commands Set door and character clearance levels

docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all clearance level configurations in the Clearance module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every clearance level configuration in the Clearance module. Each clearance level entry includes its purpose, description, and all available configuration fields with their meanings and usage.

docs.title: ClearanceLevels Configuration
docs.text: The MODULE.ClearanceLevels table defines all available clearance levels from 0 (public access) to 6 (high command access). Each level contains the following fields:

docs.title: Field Descriptions
docs.text:
• title: The display text shown when players attempt to access doors requiring this clearance level
  • pay: The salary/payment amount given to players who successfully complete clearance tasks at this level
  • desc: A detailed description of what access level this clearance provides
  • passes: The number of security passes required to bypass this clearance level
  • speed: Object containing min/max speed values for door animations
  • min: Minimum door opening/closing speed
  • max: Maximum door opening/closing speed
  • width: Object containing width settings for security indicators
  • green: Width of the green security indicator
  • blue: Width of the blue security indicator
  • warned: Boolean indicating if this clearance level shows security warnings

docs.text:

  • pay: 0
  • desc: "Public access. No clearance restrictions."
  • passes: 1

docs.title: minimumActiveTime
docs.text:
• Description: Minimum time in seconds players must be active (non-AFK) before they can claim daily rewards
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 1800 (30 minutes)
  • Purpose: Ensures players are actually playing before claiming rewards, preventing AFK abuse

docs.title: Reward Amounts
docs.text:
The rewardAmounts table defines the base reward amounts for each day of the week (1-7).

Day Reward Amount Day 1 $500 Day 2 $750 Day 3 $1000 Day 4 $1250 Day 5 $1500 Day 6 $1750 Day 7 $2500

Rewards cycle weekly, so day 8 uses day 1's reward, day 9 uses day 2's reward, etc.

docs.title: Streak Bonuses
docs.text:
The streakBonuses table defines bonus rewards for maintaining consecutive daily claim streaks.

Streak Days Bonus Reward 14 days $5,000 30 days $10,000 60 days $25,000 90 days $50,000

When players reach these milestone streaks, they receive the bonus reward in addition to their regular daily reward.

docs.title: Daily Objectives
docs.text: The objectives table defines all available objectives that players can be assigned. Each day, players receive a random selection of 3 objectives from this pool.

docs.title: Objective Properties

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

The Delivery NPC module implements a comprehensive parcel delivery side-job system that provides players with legitimate income opportunities through courier work. Players can accept delivery missions from specialized NPCs, transport packages to designated drop-off locations across the map, and earn money based on delivery distance, package value, and completion time for engaging side-employment gameplay.

Main Features
  • Delivery Jobs Accept delivery missions from NPCs
  • Delivery Crates Spawn crates that must be delivered to locations
  • Waypoint System Automatic waypoints to delivery destinations
  • Time Sensitive Optional time limits with bonus rewards
  • Delivery Trucks Special vehicles for delivery work
  • Faction System Join delivery faction to work as courier
  • Payment System Earn money for completing deliveries
  • Multiple Locations Deliver to various locations across map

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all delivery system configurations in the Delivery NPC submodule.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Delivery NPC submodule. The delivery system allows players to accept delivery jobs, transport packages to locations, and earn money with optional time-sensitive bonuses.

docs.title: Default Time-Sensitive Configuration
docs.text: The MODULE.DefaultTimeSensitiveConfig table defines default values for time-sensitive delivery bonuses when locations don't specify custom values.

docs.title: DefaultTimeSensitiveConfig Properties
docs.text:
• BaseTimeLimit: Default time limit in seconds (300 = 5 minutes)
  • TimeBonusMultiplier: Multiplier applied to base pay for time bonuses (1.5 = 50% bonus)
  • MinTimeForBonus: Minimum time remaining in seconds to qualify for bonus (60 = 1 minute)
  • TimeSensitiveBonus: Default bonus amount added to base pay (5 currency units)

docs.title: Delivery NPC Locations Configuration

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all event system configurations in the Events module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Events module. The events system allows automatic spawning of NPCs and notifications at configurable intervals to create dynamic server events.

docs.title: EventInterval
docs.text:
• Description: Time interval in seconds between event executions
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 300 (5 minutes)
  • Minimum: 60 (1 minute)
  • Maximum: 86400 (24 hours)
  • Category: Salary
  • Purpose: Controls how frequently events are triggered on the server

docs.title: Events Table
docs.text: The MODULE.EventsTable table defines all available events organized by map. Each event specifies NPCs to spawn, spawn conditions, and notification callbacks.

docs.title: How Events Work
docs.text: Events are map-specific and trigger automatically based on the EventInterval setting. When an event triggers: 1. The system checks if the event's spawn condition is met 2. If conditions are met, NPCs are spawned at specified locations 3. Notification callbacks are executed to inform players 4. The event completes and waits for the next interval

docs.title: Event Structure
docs.text: Each event contains: - NPCs: Table of NPC classes and their spawn positions - shouldSpawn: Function that determines if the event should trigger - notificationCallback: Function that sends notifications to players

docs.title: Map Organization
docs.text: Events are organized by map name. Each map can have multiple events that trigger randomly or based on conditions.

docs.title: NPC Spawn Configuration
docs.text:
NPCs are defined with their class name and an array of spawn positions:

NPCs = {
    ["npc_class_name"] = {
        Vector(x1, y1, z1),  -- Spawn position 1
        Vector(x2, y2, z2),  -- Spawn position 2
        Vector(x3, y3, z3)   -- Spawn position 3
    }
}

Multiple spawn positions allow NPCs to spawn at different locations, creating varied event experiences.

docs.title: Spawn Condition Function
docs.text:
The shouldSpawn function determines if an event should trigger:

shouldSpawn = function() 
    return player.GetCount() > 2  -- Example: require more than 2 players
end

Common conditions: - Player count checks: player.GetCount() > X - Time-based checks: os.time() > specific_time - Random chance: math.random() > 0.5 - Combination of factors

docs.title: Notification Callback Function
docs.text:
The notificationCallback function sends messages to players:

notificationCallback = function()
    for _, ply in player.Iterator() do
        if ply:isStaffOnDuty() then
            ply:notifyInfo("Staff message")
        else
            ply:notifyInfo("Player message")
        end
    end
end

Notifications can be customized to: - Send different messages to staff vs players - Target specific factions or groups - Include event-specific information - Trigger other systems

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all configuration settings in the Extraction module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Extraction module. The extraction system requires players to reach escape zones within a countdown timer, with configurable positions per map and audio/visual cues.

docs.title: ExtractionTime
docs.text:
• Description: Time in seconds for the extraction countdown timer
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 10
  • Purpose: How long players have to reach an extraction zone once the timer starts
  • Usage: Set in module.lua as MODULE.ExtractionTime = 10
  • Note: Lower values create more intense, time-pressured scenarios

docs.title: ExtractionPositions
docs.text:
• Description: Table of Vector positions where extraction zones are located
  • Type: Table (Array of Vectors)
  • Default: {Vector(-432.219879, -1589.695312, -407.968750),}
  • Purpose: Defines the world positions where players can extract
  • Usage: Set in module.lua as MODULE.ExtractionPositions = {Vector(x, y, z), ...}
  • Note:
  • Each map should have its own extraction positions configured
  • Multiple positions can be added to the table
  • Positions are typically configured per-map in the module initialization

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

The Fence NPC module implements an underground marketplace for stolen goods and contraband items, allowing players to sell illicitly obtained items to shady fence operators. These NPCs act as black market buyers who purchase stolen merchandise from players, providing an outlet for criminal activities and creating realistic underground economy mechanics for criminal roleplay scenarios.

Main Features
  • Stolen Items Only accepts items marked as stolen from robberies
  • Item Pricing Pays based on item reward value range
  • Sell Interface Sell stolen items to fence NPC for money
  • Buy Interface View available items that can be stolen
  • Robbery Integration Works with robbery system items
  • Secure Transactions Prevents selling non-stolen items

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all food and hunger system configurations in the Food module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Food module. The food system manages player hunger and thirst levels, affecting gameplay through stamina regeneration and movement restrictions.

docs.title: HungerTimer
docs.text:
• Description: Time interval in seconds between hunger decreases
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 18 seconds
  • Minimum: 0
  • Maximum: 60
  • Category: Hunger
  • Purpose: Controls how quickly players lose hunger. Lower values mean faster hunger decay.

docs.title: EnableHungerTimer
docs.text:
• Description: Whether the hunger timer system is enabled
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: true
  • Category: Hunger
  • Purpose: Allows you to completely disable hunger mechanics if desired

docs.title: HungerThreshold
docs.text:
• Description: The hunger percentage at which stamina stops regenerating
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 10%
  • Minimum: 0
  • Maximum: 100
  • Category: Hunger
  • Purpose: When hunger drops below this threshold, players cannot regenerate stamina, making them weaker in combat and movement

docs.title: RefillHungerOnDeath
docs.text:
• Description: Whether hunger refills to full when a player dies
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: true
  • Category: Hunger
  • Purpose: Controls if players respawn with full hunger or maintain their hunger level after death

docs.title: ThirstTimer
docs.text:
• Description: Time interval in seconds between thirst decreases
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 18 seconds
  • Minimum: 0
  • Maximum: 60
  • Category: Hunger (shared category)
  • Purpose: Controls how quickly players lose thirst. Lower values mean faster thirst decay.

docs.title: EnableThirstTimer
docs.text:
• Description: Whether the thirst timer system is enabled
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: true
  • Category: Hunger (shared category)
  • Purpose: Allows you to completely disable thirst mechanics if desired

docs.title: ThirstThreshold
docs.text:
• Description: The thirst percentage at which sprinting is blocked
  • Type: Integer
  • Default: 10%
  • Minimum: 0
  • Maximum: 100
  • Category: Hunger (shared category)
  • Purpose: When thirst drops below this threshold, players cannot sprint, forcing them to walk slowly

docs.title: RefillThirstOnDeath
docs.text:
• Description: Whether thirst refills to full when a player dies
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: true
  • Category: Hunger (shared category)
  • Purpose: Controls if players respawn with full thirst or maintain their thirst level after death

About

The Help NPC module implements friendly information and assistance NPCs that provide guidance, starter resources, and server information to new players. These helpful NPCs serve as welcoming interfaces that explain server mechanics, provide basic starter items, and direct players to other services while maintaining engaging conversation trees and player onboarding experiences.

Main Features
  • Information NPCs Provide guidance and server information to new players
  • Starter Items Can give basic items to help new players
  • Dialog System Conversational interface explaining server mechanics
  • Service Directory Directs players to other NPC services
  • Friendly Interface Welcoming experience for new players

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Identifications module. The identification system manages player character customization options, ID card styles, and physical attribute configurations used when generating identification documents.

docs.title: Character Customization Configuration
docs.text: The lia.identifications.CharacterCustomizationConfig table defines all customizable character attributes that appear on identification cards. Each attribute can be configured as either a dropdown menu with predefined options or a number input with min/max values.

docs.title: Eye Color (eye_color)
docs.text:
• Type: Dropdown
  • Options: Brown, Hazel, Blue, Green, Gray, Amber
  • Purpose: Sets the character's eye color displayed on ID cards

docs.title: Hair Color (hair_color)
docs.text:
• Type: Dropdown
  • Options: Bald, Brown, Blond, Black, Auburn, Red, Gray
  • Purpose: Sets the character's hair color displayed on ID cards

docs.title: Blood Type (blood_type)
docs.text:
• Type: Dropdown
  • Options: O+, O-, A+, A-, B+, B-, AB+, AB-
  • Purpose: Medical information displayed on ID cards

docs.title: Ethnicity (ethnicity)
docs.text:
• Type: Dropdown
  • Options: 50+ options including American, Italian, African, Asian, and various hyphenated combinations
  • Purpose: Sets the character's ethnic background displayed on ID cards

docs.title: Age (age)
docs.text:
• Type: Number
  • Minimum: 18
  • Maximum: 99
  • Purpose: Character's age in years

docs.title: Weight (weight)
docs.text:
• Type: Number
  • Minimum: 88 lbs
  • Maximum: 440 lbs
  • Purpose: Character's weight in pounds

docs.title: Height (height_in)
docs.text:
• Type: Dropdown
  • Options: Heights from 55 to 87 inches (4'7" to 7'3")
  • Purpose: Character's height in inches

docs.title: Background (background)
docs.text:
• Type: Dropdown
  • Options: Police, Medical, Business, Citizen
  • Purpose: Character's professional background

docs.title: Size Equivalency Table
docs.text: The lia.identifications.SizeEquivalencyTable converts height values (in centimeters) to scale multipliers used for character models. This ensures that character heights match their displayed height values.

docs.title: Layout Configuration
docs.text:
Each style's layout configuration controls where text appears on the ID card:

  • infoPanel: Controls the information panel positioning
  • rightMargin: Margin from right edge
  • top: Distance from top

  • widthFactor: Width as fraction of card width

  • data: Controls individual data field positions

  • x: Horizontal position for all fields
  • y: Vertical positions for each field (name, age, ethnicity, etc.)

  • gap: Spacing between fields

  • signature: Controls signature area positioning

  • rightPadding: Padding from right edge
  • bottomPadding: Padding from bottom edge

docs.text:
About

Advanced medical injury system with realistic damage mechanics, bleeding effects, limb injuries, and comprehensive medical weapon support for immersive roleplay scenarios.

Main Features
  • Four Injury Types Broken leg, bleeding, concussion, and pain
  • Broken Leg 50% speed reduction triggered by leg hits
  • Bleeding Variants Light and heavy bleeding with damage over time
  • Auto Heal Bleeding heals automatically after duration timer
  • Concussion Screen effects sent via network message
  • Pain 80% speed reduction until health at 100 or drug taken
  • Weapon Chances Different weapons have different injury chances
  • Fall Damage Falling can trigger broken leg with 50% chance
  • Character Storage Injuries saved to character data

docs.title: Injury Types
docs.text: The injuries system uses a configurable injury table located in config.lua. Each injury type can be customized with various properties.

docs.title: Injury Structure
docs.text:
lia.injuries.list = {
    ["injuryKey"] = {
        name = "Display Name",
        description = "Description shown to players",
        icon = "Web URL for icon",
        Icon = "Local icon path",
        chance = 25,                    -- Base chance percentage (0-100)
        chanceByFalling = 50,           -- Chance when falling (optional)
        bleedDamageAmount = 5,          -- Damage per bleed tick (bleeding only)
        weapons = {                     -- Weapon-specific chances
            ["weapon_crowbar"] = 50,    -- 50% chance when hit by crowbar
        },
        variants = {                    -- Multiple variants (bleeding only)
            ["heavy"] = {
                chance = 10,
                bleedDuration = 25,     -- How long bleeding lasts in seconds
                damageInterval = 2,     -- How often damage is applied
                weapons = {
                    ["weapon_crowbar"] = 15,
                },
            },
            ["light"] = {
                chance = 5,
                bleedDuration = 25,
                damageInterval = 2,
                weapons = {
                    ["weapon_crowbar"] = 5,
                },
            }
        },
        OnInjury = function(client, variant) -- Called when injury is applied
            -- Apply effects here
        end,
        OnHealed = function(client)      -- Called when injury is healed
            -- Remove effects here
        end,
    }
}

docs.title: Broken Leg (brokenLeg)
docs.text:
• Effect: Reduces movement speed to 50%
  • Healing: Automatic speed restoration when healed
  • Configurable: Base chance, falling chance, weapon chances

docs.title: Bleeding (bleeding)
docs.text:
• Effect: Periodic damage over time with heavy/light variants
  • Healing: Stops damage timer when healed
  • Configurable: Damage amount, duration, interval, variants

docs.title: Head Concussion (headConcussion)
docs.text:
• Effect: Screen shake and visual effects
  • Healing: Removes visual effects
  • Configurable: Base chance, weapon chances

docs.title: Pain (pain)
docs.text:
• Effect: Reduced movement speed (80% of normal)
  • Healing: Automatic or requires painkillers depending on implementation
  • Configurable: Base chance, weapon chances

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Injuries module for medical and damage systems.

Overview

The Injuries module provides advanced medical systems with limb damage, treatment mechanics, and recovery systems. It includes comprehensive health management and medical procedures. The module provides extensive hook support for customizing injury mechanics, treatment processes, recovery systems, and medical procedures.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Character leveling system with XP, skill points, and skill tree (Endurance: +25 health, Combat Training: +15% damage, Engineering: +15% tool effectiveness/+30% repair speed) with dependencies, F1 menu integration, and admin adjustment tools.

Main Features
  • XP Sources NPC kills, player kills, and passive timer
  • Friendly Kill Penalty Lose XP for killing teammates
  • Level Calculation Level calculated from total XP
  • Skill Points Gain points per level to spend on skills
  • Three Skills Endurance, Combat Training, and Engineering
  • Skill Dependencies Engineering requires Endurance tier 1
  • Passive XP Timer-based XP gain when not AFK
  • Character Storage XP, level, and skills saved to character

docs.text: The leveling system uses configurable XP values for various actions. All XP settings can be adjusted through the admin configuration panel under the "Leveling" category.

docs.title: Combat XP Settings
docs.text: Setting Description Default Range PlayerKillXP XP awarded for killing other players 30 0-1000 DefaultNPCKillXP Default XP for killing NPCs not specifically configured 15 0-1000 FriendlyKillPenalty XP penalty for killing friendly players -20 0-1000 ReprimandPenalty XP penalty for administrative reprimands -50 0-1000

docs.text: Setting Description Default Range XPBarFadeInTime Time for XP bar to fade in (seconds) 0.5 0.1-5.0 XPBarDisplayTime How long XP bar stays visible (seconds) 5.0 1.0-30.0 XPBarFadeOutTime Time for XP bar to fade out (seconds) 1.0 0.1-10.0 XPBarWidth Width of XP bar in pixels 600 200-800 XPBarHeight Height of XP bar in pixels 50 10-50

docs.title: NPC XP Configuration
docs.text:
Specific NPCs can be configured to give custom XP amounts. This is done by editing the MODULE.NPCKillXP table in config.lua:

MODULE.NPCKillXP = {
    ["npc_vj_crabsynth2_z"] = 100,  -- Example: Custom NPC gives 100 XP
    ["npc_combine_soldier"] = 25,   -- Combine soldiers give 25 XP
}

docs.title: Skill Definitions
docs.text:
Skills are defined in module.lua and can be customized. Each skill has the following properties:

skillName = {
    name = "Display Name",
    description = "Description shown to players",
    image = "path/to/enabled/icon.png",
    imageDisabled = "path/to/disabled/icon.png",
    uiPosition = { x = 400, y = 200 },  -- Position in skill tree UI
    cost = 2,                           -- Skill points required
    requirement = 5,                    -- Level requirement
    dependsOn = {                       -- Skills required (optional)
        { skill = "otherSkill", tier = 1 }
    },
    -- Skill-specific bonuses:
    bonus = 25,                         -- Health bonus for endurance
    damage = 1.15,                      -- Damage multiplier for combat
    repairSpeed = 1.3,                  -- Repair speed multiplier
    toolEffectiveness = 1.15            -- Tool effectiveness multiplier
}

docs.title: Engineering
docs.text:

  • Cost: 3 skill points
  • Dependencies: Endurance (tier 1)
  • Effects: +15% tool effectiveness, +30% repair speed

docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Limb Damage module. The limb damage system causes leg shots to prevent sprinting for a configurable duration, with automatic healing and toggleable enable/disable functionality.

docs.title: Configuration Options
docs.text: These configuration options are registered via lia.config.add() and can be accessed using lia.config.get():

docs.text:
• Description: How long leg damage prevents sprinting (in seconds)

  • Default: 4
  • Min: 1
  • Max: 30
  • Category: "Limb Damage"
  • Usage: lia.config.get("LegDamageDelay", 4)
  • Purpose: Controls the duration that players cannot sprint after taking leg damage

docs.title: LegDamageEnabled
docs.text:
• Description: Whether the leg damage system is enabled
  • Type: Boolean
  • Default: true
  • Category: "Limb Damage"
  • Usage: lia.config.get("LegDamageEnabled", true)
  • Purpose: Master toggle to enable or disable the entire limb damage system

  • Door Vehicles Works on doors and vehicles

  • Level Reset Lockpick level resets when door purchased
  • Lockpick Ammo Consumable lockpick pins as ammo items

About

  • Container Inventories Each container has LootInventory
  • Item Transfer Drag and drop items to and from containers

  • Luck Bonus 2% bonus per luck attribute, max 50%
  • Persistence Containers save state across server restarts
  • Lock Duration Containers have lockTime cooldown
  • Alarm System Containers can trigger alarms
  • Admin Spawn Spawn loot containers via command

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Comprehensive documentation for all looting system configurations in the Looting module.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This file contains detailed documentation for every configuration setting in the Looting module. The looting system allows players to search containers and props in the world to find items, with various difficulty levels and security systems.

docs.title: Lootable Props Configuration
docs.text: The MODULE.LootableProps table defines all containers and props that players can loot. Each prop has properties controlling difficulty, rewards, and security features.

docs.title: Prop Properties
docs.text: Each lootable prop contains the following configuration options:

docs.title: Basic Properties
docs.text:
• name: Display name shown to players
  • model: 3D model path for the prop
  • chance: Percentage chance (0-100) that the prop contains loot
  • chanceTime: Time in seconds between chance checks
  • spawnChance: Percentage chance (0-100) that the prop spawns in the world

docs.title: Skill Requirements
docs.text:
• strength: Minimum strength attribute required
  • luck: Minimum luck attribute required
  • luckChance: Percentage chance that luck helps bypass requirements
  • intelligenceLock: Whether intelligence is required to unlock (for safes)

docs.title: Interaction Properties
docs.text:
• openingSound: Sound file played when prop is opened
  • forceAnimation: Whether player must perform an animation to open
  • lockDuration: Time in seconds the prop stays locked after being opened

docs.title: Alarm System
docs.text:
• enabled: Whether alarm system is active
  • detectionChance: Percentage chance alarm triggers when opened
  • alarmSound: Sound file played when alarm triggers
  • lightEffect: Whether visual light effect appears
  • npcSpawn: Whether NPCs spawn when alarm triggers

docs.title: Item Tables
docs.text: Items are organized by rarity tiers with different drop chances: - Common: Most common items, highest chance - Uncommon: Less common items, medium chance - Rare: Rare items, lower chance - Legendary: Very rare items, very low chance - Unique: Extremely rare items, minimal chance

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

LSCS (Lightsaber Combat System) compatibility module that integrates lightsaber components into the Helix inventory system, enabling players to equip hilts, crystals, stances, and Force abilities through the standard item interface with dual-wielding support.

Main Features
  • LSCS Integration Compatibility with Lightsaber Combat System addon
  • Inventory Items Hilts, crystals, stances, and Force abilities as inventory items
  • Dual Wielding Equip hilts and crystals to right or left hand separately
  • Hand Occupancy Check Prevents equipping multiple items to same hand
  • Saber Crafting Automatically crafts weapon_lscs when items equipped
  • Inventory Sync Wipes LSCS inventory on character load
  • Visual Indicators Green checkmarks show equipped status per hand
  • Item Categories Abilities, Crystals, Hilts, and Stances

About

Provides a marketplace system for player-to-player trading with no expiration dates. Features configurable listing limits, minimum prices, NPC vendor interface, search and filter functionality, and seamless economic system integration for enhanced server economy.

Main Features
  • Item Listings Players can list items for sale through NPC
  • No Expiration Listings never expire and stay active indefinitely
  • Listing Limits Maximum 10 listings per player configurable
  • Minimum Price Enforces minimum price requirement for listings
  • Search Function Find specific items quickly in marketplace
  • Page System Browse listings with pagination
  • Admin Removal Super admins can remove items from marketplace
  • Database Storage All listings saved in database
  • Item Filtering Filter items by type and category

Overview

The Medals module provides achievement and recognition systems with various medal types, earning criteria, and display systems. It includes comprehensive achievement tracking and reward systems. The module provides extensive hook support for customizing medal criteria, earning mechanics, display systems, and achievement tracking.

docs.text:
About

Roleplay NPC framework with dialogue trees, quest systems, and submodule support (mechanics, taxis, vendors, fences) with centralized NPC management and customization tools.

Main Features
  • NPC Framework Modular system for custom NPCs
  • Dialogue Trees Branching conversation systems
  • Quest System NPCs provide and track quests
  • Easy Creation Simple configuration for new NPCs
  • Centralized Management Unified NPC script system
  • Vehicle Dealers Car purchasing and garage management
  • Property Management Real estate and door ownership
  • Repair Services Mechanic NPCs for vehicle repair
  • Black Market Underground economy NPCs
  • Delivery System Courier NPCs with waypoints

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Comprehensive police system with alarms, 999 emergency calls, stun weapons, crime tracking, warrants, arrests, police NPCs, and computer database for law enforcement management.

Main Features
  • Door Alarms Doors trigger alarms when opened
  • ECM Jammer Criminals can jam police communications with ecm_jammer item
  • Emergency Responders Get responders by faction ID
  • Police Registry Character registry tracks warrants and arrests
  • Warrants System Create and track warrants in registry
  • Wanted Status Shows if player has active warrants
  • Arrest System Arrest handcuffed players with duration
  • Prisoner Tracking activePrisoners table tracks jailed players
  • Jailer NPCs NPCs for managing prisoners
  • Police Computer Database system for viewing records
  • Stun Baton Weapon that stuns players
  • Panic Button 999 emergency call system
